title: The Rank-for-LLMs playbook (2026 edition)
description: What we changed in our SEO process the moment ChatGPT became a search engine.
content:
Why LLM citation is the new first-page ranking

In 2024, ChatGPT passed 100M daily active users conducting search-like queries. By 2026, more B2B buying research happens inside LLMs than on Google for several verticals. If your brand isn't cited, you don't exist to that buyer.

The three pillars

1. Entity authority

LLMs are trained on the web but reason about entities — named things with attributes and relationships. Build entity-rich content that states facts clearly: who you are, what you do, proof points with numbers.

2. Topical coverage

You need dense, interlinked coverage of your category. One great article isn't enough. LLMs favor sources that cover a topic comprehensively from multiple angles.

3. Citation surface

Get cited in places LLMs trust: Reddit threads, product forums, LinkedIn, industry publications. These are the citation surfaces that feed training data and retrieval systems.

title: The 90-day GTM checklist for Series A
description: Every milestone, every miss-and-fix-it pattern we've seen.
content:
The GTM clock starts at term sheet

Series A investors expect marketing to be a growth lever within 90 days. Most founders wait too long to hire, then hire wrong. This checklist fixes that.

Week 1–2: Input

Interview 20 customers. Listen for the exact words they use to describe their problem. Don't invent language — steal theirs. Map your ICP scoring rubric before you touch the website.

Week 3–4: Write

Positioning narrative. One page. If it takes more than one page you don't have clarity yet. Ship v1 to your best customer and ask: does this sound like you?

Week 5–8: Ship

Website, outbound sequence, one paid channel. Not all three at once — pick the highest-signal channel for your ICP and go deep. Measure weekly.

Week 9–12: Scale

Double down on what's working. Fire the channels that aren't by week eight — don't let them limp to week twelve.
